data.content:
# Implementation handoff

**Branch**: `feature/knots-db35-delete-knotstate` (pushed to origin)

**Commits**:
- `9730baf` — Add state_aliases to ProfileDefinition and populate builtin profiles
- `0272771` — Replace KnotState enum with workflow-driven helpers
- `1d8b823` — Migrate state consumers off KnotState to string-based checks
- `9380ab1` — Drop AppError::ParseState and InvalidTransition variants

**Verification**: `make sanity` passes. Coverage 95.25% (≥ 95% threshold), +0.00% change. `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and full `cargo test` all green.

**Files changed**:
- `src/profile.rs` — new `state_aliases: BTreeMap<String, String>` field on `ProfileDefinition`
- `src/installed_workflows/builtin.rs` — populates aliases per builtin profile (only when target state is in that profile's `states`)
- `src/installed_workflows/profile_json.rs`, `profile_toml.rs` — default-empty `state_aliases` on construction
- `src/domain/state.rs` — enum deleted; replaced with `normalize_state_input`, `resolve_state`, `is_terminal`, `can_transition`, `rank` as workflow-driven free functions
- `src/tiering.rs`, `src/state_hierarchy.rs`, `src/write_dispatch/helpers.rs`, `src/profile_commands.rs` — migrated off `KnotState::from_str` to profile-driven / string-based checks
- `src/app/error.rs` — removed `ParseState` and `InvalidTransition` variants (now covered by `ProfileError`)
- `src/app/tests_error_paths.rs`, `tests_coverage_ext.rs`, `tests_exploration.rs` — updated to assert `ProfileError` paths

**Acceptance check**:
- ✅ `src/domain/state.rs` no longer defines enum or hardcoded state list / alias map / terminal set / transition table
- ✅ State validation, terminality, rank, and transitions query the installed `ProfileDefinition`
- ✅ Legacy aliases (idea, work_item, implemented, approved, refining, rejected, etc.) live in `state_aliases`, not Rust match arms
- ✅ Installing a workflow with a new state name requires no Rust edit — new-state parity test asserts this
- ✅ `make sanity` passes; coverage holds at 95.25%

Ready for implementation review.
